main_sections.title: Conquering the Summit
main_sections.prose_summary: Conquering the Summit is an informational biography of Arunima Sinha, the first Indian female amputee to climb Mount Everest. In April 2011, Arunima — then 24, a national-level volleyball player from Ambedkar Nagar, Uttar Pradesh — was pushed from a moving train by thieves and lost her leg. Despite the devastating trauma, she made a vow while in hospital: to climb Everest with a prosthetic leg. She completed a basic mountaineering course at the Nehru Institute of Mountaineering in Uttarkashi, underwent eighteen months of rigorous training, and sought mentorship from Bachendri Pal, the first Indian woman to summit Everest. On 21 May 2013, after 52 exhausting days, Arunima reached the summit, displaying the Indian flag and leaving behind a photograph of Swami Vivekananda. She was awarded the Padma Shri (2015), the Tenzing Norgay National Adventure Award, and National Award for Best Female Mountaineer. By 2019 she had completed all Seven Summits, becoming the world's first female amputee to climb Mount Vinson in Antarctica. Her autobiography Born Again on the Mountain records this journey. Her central message — 'Never give up and work hard' — and Bachendri Pal's words ('you have conquered the Everest within you') frame the piece's themes of resilience, mental fortitude, and the power of a positive mindset.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read. (I) Identify the activities represented by the icons given below. Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher. (II) Work in pairs and discuss: (1) Why do you think people undertake these activities? (2) How would they prepare for these activities? (3) Which one do you find the most exciting? Why? (III) Work in pairs to complete the word web: Qualities of a mountaineer.

question_bank.stem: Work in pairs and discuss: (1) Why do you think people undertake adventure sports/mountaineering? (2) How would they prepare for these activities? (3) Which one of them do you find the most exciting? Why?
Complete the word web: Qualities of a mountaineer.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss. Read the statements given below and explain each statement in your own words.

question_bank.stem: Explain in your own words: 'Arunima Sinha is considered a beacon of hope and resilience.'
Explain in your own words: 'Arunima decided to climb Mount Everest despite losing her leg.'
Explain in your own words: 'Bachendri Pal's guidance was crucial in Arunima's journey to the summit of Mount Everest.'
Explain in your own words: 'Arunima's achievements go beyond just physical endurance.'
Explain in your own words: 'Arunima's autobiography inspires readers around the world.'

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — Section I. Read the extract (Bachendri Pal's words to Arunima + Arunima's summit day) and answer the questions. Section II — Answer the following questions on the full text.

question_bank.stem: Bachendri Pal said that Arunima had conquered the Everest within her because ___________________.
How might Arunima have felt after what Bachendri Pal told her?
Choose a phrase from the extract which shows that reaching the summit of Mount Everest was tiring.

After her success when Arunima felt 'an overwhelming sense of achievement', she would have most likely said, "___________________.". (i) I am confident of my abilities (ii) I will accomplish this soon (iii) I am excited to begin this climb (iv) I finally realised my dream
options: I am confident of my abilities
I am excited to begin this climb

question_bank.stem: What did Arunima do in order to fulfill her ambition of conquering Mount Everest?
Arunima is a beacon of hope and resilience. Support this statement with evidence from the text.
Arunima's autobiography is titled Born Again on the Mountain: A Story of Losing Everything and Finding It Back. Why do you think this title is appropriate?
Arunima says, 'Failure is when we don't have goals worthy enough.' What would be the most likely impact of this statement on readers?

listening.transcript_text:
Speaker (i): As a parent, I fear the risks involved, especially with activities like rock climbing or paragliding. I'm quite worried about the safety risks of adventure sports. It's important to consider these risks before letting children participate.
Speaker (ii): Adventure sports are thrilling! All my friends agree; it is amazing. It's not just about fun; it's about pushing your limits and experiencing something new. I think it's worth trying, of course, with a trainer.
Speaker (iii): As organisers of adventure camps, we take safety very seriously. Our team ensures all equipment is regularly checked and maintained. Participants are given thorough training and are always supervised by professionals. We focus on minimising risks while still providing an exciting experience.
Speaker (iv): At our school, we organise adventure camps with activities like trekking and zip-lining. However, we stick to basic sports that are safe and manageable for students. We always prioritise safety, ensuring that all necessary precautions and measures are in place. Being careful is what we promote.
Speaker (v): In my opinion, sports should be about fitness and enjoyment, like track and field or swimming. Why risk your life for a fleeting moment of thrill just for a feeling of adventure? Traditional sports are safer and just as fulfilling!

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to five speakers share their opinion about adventure sports. As you listen match the statements 1–8 with the speakers (i)–(v). There are three extra statements that you do not need.

question_bank.stem: Match statements 1–8 with speakers (i)–(v). There are three extra statements. Statements: (1) Talks about having basic activities in adventure sports. (2) Finds adventure sports thrilling and exciting. (3) Praises the availability of a variety of activities. (4) Expresses concern about the risks in adventure sports. (5) Promotes trekking as a casual sport. (6) Invites applications from first-timers. (7) Focuses on the availability of training and supervision. (8) Finds exposure to risks unnecessary.
question_bank.table:
Statement|Speaker
Talks about having basic activities in adventure sports|iv
Finds adventure sports thrilling and exciting|ii
Expresses concern about the risks in adventure sports|i
Focuses on the availability of training and supervision|iii
Finds exposure to risks unnecessary|v

tasks_verbatim.task_text: In groups of four, select a role from 1–4 and speak about the views allotted to each using the prompts and vocabulary given. Take turns to express your opinions, agreement, and disagreement. Roles: (1) School Student — why Physical Education should be included in school activities; (2) Parent — concerns or support for child participating in PE; (3) Physical Education Teacher — share experiences and value of PE activities; (4) Sports Camp Organiser — explain how you ensure safety and why sports are beneficial.

Read the various components of a formal letter from the box given below (complimentary close, receiver's address, subject, date, introduction, statement of request, expression of interest, salutation, signature line, sender's address). (1) Read the letter given below and write the matching components. (2) Write a letter to the Principal of your school requesting her to organise an excursion to a nearby hill station for Grade 7 students of your school.

question_bank.stem: Read the sample formal letter (Request to join Adventure Club from Sreelakshmi, Grade 7-C) and identify and label each component (sender's address, date, receiver's address, subject, salutation, introduction, statement of request, expression of interest, complimentary close, signature line).
Write a formal letter to the Principal of your school requesting her to organise an excursion to a nearby hill station for Grade 7 students of your school.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the underlined phrases in sentences (i)–(v) and match them with their appropriate meanings from the box (genuine, sincere efforts / strength of mind / periods of time filled with tiring and demanding activities / an incident or experience that significantly changes a person's life / a source of inspiration or encouragement).

question_bank.stem: Match the underlined phrases with their meanings: (i) 'several exhausting days of practice' (ii) 'heartfelt attempts' (iii) 'life-altering event' (iv) 'mental fortitude' (v) 'beacon of hope'.
question_bank.table:
Phrase|Meaning
exhausting days|periods of time filled with tiring and demanding activities
heartfelt attempts|genuine, sincere efforts
life-altering event|an incident or experience that significantly changes a person's life
mental fortitude|strength of mind
beacon of hope|a source of inspiration or encouragement

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the following table by filling in the gaps with correct parts of speech. (Nouns, Adjectives, Verbs (present), Adverbs — for: determination, relief, inspiring/inspire, resistance, success.)

question_bank.stem:
Complete the word-form table:
| Noun | Adjective | Verb (present) | Adverb |
| determination | determined | determine | determinedly |
| relief | ___ | ___ | relievedly |
| ___ | ___ | inspire | ___ |
| resistance | ___ | ___ | resistantly |
| success | successful | ___ | ___ |

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Study the highlighted articles (a, an, the) in the sentences from the text. 'A' and 'an' are indefinite articles; 'the' is the definite article. The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on the initial sound of the word that follows.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks with 'a', 'an', 'the', or 'x' (where no article is required): 'We planned (i) _______ adventurous trip to (ii) _______ remote island. (iii) _______ island was known for its beautiful landscapes and (iv) _______ diverse wildlife. On (v) _______ first day, we set out to explore (vi) _______ island's dense jungle. (vii) _______ guide led us through (viii) _______ thick foliage and showed us (ix) _______ hidden waterfall. In (x) _______ evening, we gathered around (xi) _______ campfire to share stories and enjoy (xii) _______ meal we cooked. (xiii) _______ experience was truly unforgettable, and we felt (xiv) _______ connection with (xv) _______ nature.'

Correct the article error in each sentence: (i) We waited for a hour before the bus finally arrived. (ii) The guide insisted on an uniform jacket for the group's safety during the trek. (iii) Mount K2 is a second highest peak in the world. (iv) A Ganga is a sacred river in India, known for its cultural importance. (v) Challenges are faced with courage and determination by a brave. (vi) A North Star has guided travellers for centuries.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Find out more information about the following adventure sports in India and complete the table given below (Name, Area, Preferred Time of the Year, Equipment Required): snow skiing, trekking, camping, river rafting, scuba diving.

question_bank.stem: Find out more information about these adventure sports in India and complete the table (Name, Area, Preferred Time of the Year, Equipment Required): snow skiing (snow-covered hills; skis, ski suit), trekking (Spring, Autumn, Winter; pole), camping (grassy areas; tent, torch), river rafting (all year round), scuba diving (scuba suit, mask, fins).

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Prepare an information booklet with fact sheets of five Indian mountaineers. Include the following information in each fact sheet: (1) name and photograph if available; (2) place of birth; (3) profession; (4) accomplishments — awards or honours; (5) training received; (6) sport known for (if any).

question_bank.stem: Prepare an information booklet with fact sheets of five Indian mountaineers, covering: name and photograph, place of birth, profession, accomplishments/awards, training received, and sport known for.
